commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=601b77202711d5b3d95c9208edba339e3ec4063f)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes trusted tool policy execution and contract tests to defensively read policy registrations, evaluator results, decisions, and adjusted params, returning deterministic blocking results for malformed trusted-policy data.

PR surface: Source +228, Tests +216. Total +444 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection on current main shows direct trusted-policy registry and evaluator reads, and the PR adds focused contract cases for the malformed/unreadable states; I did not execute tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] This protected maintainer draft needs a small sibling-path fix plus human acceptance of the compatibility-sensitive fail-closed behavior, not an automated cleanup close.

target.title: fix(plugins): fail closed on trusted policy errors
target.body:
## Summary

- Fails closed when plugin-owned trusted tool policy registration, policy list, evaluator, decision, or adjusted params are unreadable, throwing, or malformed.
- Keeps healthy trusted-policy chaining behavior intact for normal approvals, blocks, and adjusted params.
- Intentionally scoped to runtime trusted-policy execution. Broader fuzz/registration hardening remains out of scope for this PR.

## Linked context

Related #87141

Requested by maintainer as a pruned critical-hardening slice from the larger fuzzing branch.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: malformed or throwing trusted tool policy data can no longer fail open, silently skip the policy gate, or reject before the deterministic fail-closed path.
- Real environment tested: final rebased local focused Vitest wrapper; AWS Crabbox changed gate on the pushed patch surface.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/plugins/contracts/host-hooks.contract.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
  - `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/plugins/trusted-tool-policy.ts src/plugins/contracts/host-hooks.contract.test.ts`
  - `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
  - private-name/private-path branch scrub
  - `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`
  - `OPENCLAW_TESTBOX=1 node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"`
- Evidence after fix: focused contract test passed 67 tests on final rebased head `601b7720271`; oxfmt passed; diff check passed; branch scrub found no private paths or private reported names; autoreview was clean; AWS Crabbox `run_df20797f60e2` on lease `cbx_560b55a148cc` ran `pnpm check:changed` and exited 0.
- Observed result after fix: thrown evaluators, unreadable policy lists, unreadable registrations, unreadable decisions, malformed object decisions, malformed falsey primitive decisions, unreadable decision fields, and unreadable adjusted params all return deterministic blocking results instead of allowing or crashing through the tool call path.
- What was not tested: a live packaged third-party plugin invoking this path.

## Tests and validation

Commands run:

- `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/plugins/contracts/host-hooks.contract.test.ts --reporter=verbose` (67 tests passed)
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/plugins/trusted-tool-policy.ts src/plugins/contracts/host-hooks.contract.test.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- private-name/private-path branch scrub
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main` (clean)
- `OPENCLAW_TESTBOX=1 node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"` (AWS Crabbox `run_df20797f60e2`, lease `cbx_560b55a148cc`, exit 0)

Regression coverage added:

- trusted policy evaluator throws
- unreadable trusted policy list accessor
- revoked trusted policy list proxy
- unreadable trusted policy registration
- unreadable policy decision object
- malformed truthy primitive decision
- malformed falsey primitive decisions: `false`, `null`, `0`, and empty string
- malformed object decisions: array, date, function, empty object, non-boolean `allow`, and non-boolean `block`
- unreadable adjusted params

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. Malformed trusted policy plugin data now blocks tool calls instead of failing open or crashing the policy path.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. This tightens tool execution policy handling at the trusted-policy boundary.

What is the highest-risk area?

A malformed trusted policy that previously no-oped now blocks tool execution with a deterministic reason.

How is that risk mitigated?

The behavior is limited to unreadable, throwing, or malformed plugin-owned policy data. Normal allow/block/approval/params chaining is covered by the existing contract tests plus the new fail-closed cases.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Draft PR for maintainer review and CI.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

GitHub CI and maintainer review.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

Local autoreview found malformed object/list fail-open or pre-guard rejection gaps; this PR now blocks those cases and includes regression coverage.
target.author: vincentkoc

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
